macro_segments.title: The Mishna on Blessings and Shofar Blasts: Framework and Assumptions

micro_segments.title: Opening: The Final Daf of Masechet Rosh Hashanah
Mishna: Malchuyos, Zichronos, and Shofros as Blessings Independent of Shofar Blasts
Gemara: The Reason—Shofar as Primary, Blessings as Secondary
Rav Pappa bar Shmuel's Private Davening with Signaled Shofar Blasts
Rava's Restriction: Shofar Blasts During Shemoneh Esrei Only with a Minyan

macro_segments.title: The Braita on Communal vs. Individual Obligations and the Role of the Shliach Tzibur

micro_segments.title: Braita: Hearing Shofar Blasts in Order with the Blessings (Bechaver Ir vs. Shelo Bechaver Ir)
Individual Fulfillment Through Others: Shofar Blasts Yes, Blessings No
The Priority of Shofar Over Blessings: A Biblical vs. Rabbinic Distinction
Rashi's Position: Malchuyos, Zichronos, and Shofros as Integral to D'oraisa Shofar Blasts
The Communal Context: Why Shofar and Blessings Work Together Only with a Minyan

macro_segments.title: The Debate between Rabban Gamliel and the Rabbis: The Shliach Tzibur's Role in Shemoneh Esrei

micro_segments.title: Braita: Rabban Gamliel Claims the Shliach Tzibur Motzi Everyone; Rabbis Disagree
Rabban Gamliel's Justification: Private Davening Serves Only to Help the Shliach Tzibur Prepare
The Rabbis' Counter: The Shliach Tzibur Only Motzi Those Who Cannot Daven (Eino Baki)
Rabban Gamliel's Response: The Same Principle Applies to Both Eino Baki and Baki
Rabbi Yochanan's Report: The Rabbis Conceded to Rabban Gamliel (Modim Chachamim)

macro_segments.title: The Dispute Clarified: Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's Resolution and the Limited Scope of Paskening

micro_segments.title: Did Rabbi Yochanan Really Claim the Rabbis Conceded? Rav Nachman's Alternative Reading
Rav Chana Tzippora's Clarification: The Paskening Is Limited to Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur Blessings
Rabba bar Bar Chana's Source: A Different Braita with Rabbi Meir Agreeing with Rabban Gamliel
The Actual Position: We Rule Like Rabban Gamliel, But the Debate Continues

macro_segments.title: Why Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur Are Special: The Length and Complexity of the Blessings

micro_segments.title: The Question: Why Are These Days Different from Ordinary Days?
First Proposal: Many Verses in Malchuyos, Zichronos, and Shofros (Refuted)
Rav Chananel's Answer: One Can Substitute 'V'Toratcha Katuv Leimor' for the Verses
Corrected Answer: The True Problem Is the Abundance of Blessings (Avshei Brachot)
The Halachic Issue: Can One Avoid Citing Specific Verses When Saying Malchuyos, Zichronos, and Shofros?
Tosafot's Approach: A Choice Between Citing Verses or Using Summary Formulas
HaRan's Approach: Interpreting the Shortcut as Consistent with the Three-Verse Position
Rashi's Approach: The Verse Problem Applies to Musaf, Not to Malchuyos, Zichronos, and Shofros
Tosafot's Counter to Rashi: Musaf Requires Verses Because It Replaces the Offering
The Rosh Chodesh Musaf Issue: Must It Be Mentioned, and How?

macro_segments.title: Preparation and Knowledge: Individual Preparation vs. Communal Standardization

micro_segments.title: Rav Elazar's Principle: Always Order Your Prayer Mentally Before Davening
Rav Abba's Qualification: This Applies Only to Infrequent Prayers or Special Occasions
The Objection: Rav Yehuda Would Order His Prayer Daily—How Is That Consistent?
The Resolution: Rav Yehuda Davened Only Once a Month, Making It Like a Yom Tov for Him

macro_segments.title: Rabban Gamliel's Exemption: The Scope of Communal Prayer and Who Is Included

micro_segments.title: Rabban Gamliel Exempts Even Those in the Fields from Individual Obligation
Initial Objection: Those in the City Who Stay Home Are Separating from the Community
The Distinction Clarified: Exemption Applies Only to Those Unable to Attend (Anisei Malacha)
Prerequisites for Communal Inclusion: Must Attend the Entire Davening from Start to Finish
The Theological Basis: Communal Prayer as Tefillat HaTzibur, Not Shomea K'Oneh

topical_tags.term: Malchuyos, Zichronos, Shofros (Blessings of Kingship, Remembrance, and Shofar Blasts)
Shofar blasts (Tekiot) as D'oraisa vs. blessings as D'rabbanan
Minyan (community) requirement for integrating blessings with shofar blasts
Rashi's position: Malchuyos integral to D'oraisa shofar obligation when done together
Shliach Tzibur (cantor) and the question of whether he motzi (releases from obligation) the congregation
Rabban Gamliel vs. Rabbis: Debate on communal prayer and individual obligation
Eino baki (one unable to daven) vs. baki (one who can daven)
Shomea k'oneh (hearing as answering): whether one can fulfill obligation by listening
Tefillat HaTzibur (communal prayer) vs. Tefillat Yachid (individual prayer)
Rabbi Yochanan vs. Rav: Did the Rabbis concede to Rabban Gamliel?
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak: Alternative reading distinguishing Rabbi Meir from the Rabbis
Limited scope of ruling: Paskening like Rabban Gamliel applies to Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur blessings only
Avshei Brachot (abundance of blessings): The true reason for the Rosh Hashanah exception
Verses in Malchuyos, Zichronos, Shofros: Minimum requirement and alternatives
Tosafot: Two tracks—either cite verses or use summary formulas
HaRan: Three-verse principle (one from Torah, Ketuvim, Nevi'im) underlying the shortcut
Rashi: Verse issues apply to Musaf, not to Malchuyos, Zichronos, Shofros
Musaf (Additional Service) and the requirement to recite specific verses
Rosh Chodesh (New Month) Musaf: Whether to mention it and implications for emphasis
Rav Elazar's principle: Mental ordering (Asdir) of prayer before davening
Special occasions (Yom Tov, Rosh Hashanah, Yom Kippur) requiring preparation
Rav Yehuda: Exception to the preparation rule due to infrequent davening
Rabban Gamliel's exemption: Those in the fields (or unable to attend) from individual obligation
Anisei Malacha (compelled by labor): Exemption criterion for those unable to attend synagogue
Full attendance requirement: Must hear the entire davening from beginning to end
Poresh min HaTzibur (separating from the community): Those who could attend but don't
